chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Constitution Petition No. D-850 of 2019 (Farman Ali Vs. Federation of Sindh & others) DATE OF HEARING ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For Orders on office objection. 2. For hearing of main case. 07-03-2024. Mr. Sajjad Muhammad Zangejo, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Karim Bux Janwari, Assistant Attorney General, Pakistan. ******** 1. Over ruled. 2. The petitioner by way of instant petition has sought for directions against the respondents i) to implement the order Electric Inspector Sukkur; ii) withdraw the notice issued against him under the NAB Ordinance, iii) refrain from disconnecting his electric supply and iv) to take legal action against the officials through FIA, who are responsible to impose illegal and unjustified detection bill upon him. No provision of law is referred which may authorize this Court to implement an order passed by Electric Inspector Sukkur. If a notice has been issued against the petitioner under the NAB Ordinance then he has remedy to contest the same; therefore, same could not be directed to be withdrawn summarily by this Court. If the petitioner is having a feeling that some wrong has been done to him by the officials then he has remedy to sue them for that wrong in accordance with law without seeking directions from this Court against any agency. The consumption of electricity is subject to payment of bills, if the bills are paid regularly, then issue of disconnection could hardly arise. No case for indulgence by this Court by way of instant petition is made out, it is dismissed accordingly. Judge Judge 2 Nasim/P.A
